top_strategic.title: Sponsorship whitespace: 5 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection
top_strategic.evidence: The camera sees these items but we detect no brands. ZERO detections: accessories, apparel, footwear, sunglasses, wristband. Near-zero: hat(7). Each gap = a brand that could be paying for visibility data.
top_strategic.action: Add shoe/apparel/sunglasses brand detection examples to Gemini prompt — immediate revenue unlock

top_strategic.title: Endorsement intelligence: 162 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences
top_strategic.evidence: Top pairs: PickleBill-JOOLA(703x), Chintan-JOOLA(436x), Daniella-JOOLA(322x), Coach_Block-JOOLA(200x), Tkjacobs-JOOLA(193x)
top_strategic.action: Build 'brand ambassador identification' product for equipment companies

top_strategic.title: Longitudinal tracking possible: 54 players with 5+ clips
top_strategic.evidence: 54 players have enough data for improvement tracking. Top: PickleBill(411), Chintan(262), Daniella(110), Coach_Block(106), Ironvarr(88)
top_strategic.action: Build player improvement trajectory product — track skill changes over time

top_strategic.title: Sponsorship whitespace detected in 1461 clips (92.0%) — sell to brands
top_strategic.evidence: We detect unbranded equipment/space in 1461 clips. This is the gap between what's visible and what's branded — sellable data.
top_strategic.action: Package sponsorship whitespace data as 'brand opportunity report' for equipment companies

top_strategic.title: Reverse Sponsorship Auction
top_strategic.evidence: Instead of brands chasing players, players auction their court-time to brands. A rising amateur player in NYC gets 5 minutes of video with JOOLA equipment, Courtana captures it, brands bid on the resulting clips. Players earn micropayments ($5-50 per clip), brands get authentic grassroots content, we handle curation and quality control. Viral loop: good players get more bids, motivates improvement, feeds our analytics.

top_data.title: Viral shot: 'overhead' clips average 5.67 viral vs 'none' at 1
top_data.evidence: Shot viral averages: overhead=5.67, reset=4, volley=3.84, dink=3.13, drive=2.99, serve=1.44, none=1
top_data.action: Prioritize highlight reels featuring high-viral shot types

top_data.title: Prompt fix: 5 badges consistently missed — add positive examples
top_data.evidence: Production awards these but DaaS misses them: 'rally master' (6 missed), 'new look' (5 missed), 'steady eddie' (4 missed), 'paddle tap celebration' (3 missed), 'fresh fit' (3 missed). These are real badges that our prompt doesn't detect.
top_data.action:
Add positive examples to Gemini prompt:
'When analyzing badges, make sure to check for these commonly missed badges:
  - rally master: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - new look: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - steady eddie: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - paddle tap celebration: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - fresh fit: Look for [describe visual pattern]

top_data.title: DaaS badge prediction: F1=0.97 (precision=0.98, recall=0.96)
top_data.evidence: Across 287 verified clips: 999 correct predictions, 16 false positives, 41 missed badges. Overall F1=0.972.
top_data.action: Target recall improvement: add more badge examples to Gemini prompt

top_data.title: Net/venue branding inflates 3 brands — true share-of-court differs from raw counts
top_data.evidence: Brands with significant venue-only detections (not product use): JOOLA (257/2761 are venue), PUSHIT (3/6 are venue), Nicol (3/8 are venue). Raw counts overstate market share. Product-use only: JOOLA=2504, Nike=267, PICKLEBALL=92, HEAD=64, On=31.
top_data.action: Report TRUE share-of-court (product-use only) to brand partners, not raw detection counts

top_data.title: Viral formula: quality (7.59 vs 5.42) + watchability (6.45 vs 3.14)
top_data.evidence: Top 100 viral clips: quality=7.59, watchability=6.45. Bottom 100: quality=5.42, watchability=3.14. Both metrics track strongly with viral potential.
top_data.action: Use (quality + watchability) / 2 as quick viral prediction score

all_findings.title: Sponsorship whitespace: 5 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection
all_findings.evidence: The camera sees these items but we detect no brands. ZERO detections: accessories, apparel, footwear, sunglasses, wristband. Near-zero: hat(7). Each gap = a brand that could be paying for visibility data.
all_findings.action: Add shoe/apparel/sunglasses brand detection examples to Gemini prompt — immediate revenue unlock

all_findings.title: Prompt fix: JOOLA appears in 215/287 verified clips — add disambiguation
all_findings.evidence: JOOLA dominates 75% of clips. Prompt should distinguish: paddle logo (real JOOLA use), net logo (JOOLA sponsors nets), and venue branding (JOOLA sponsors the facility).
all_findings.action:
Add to brand detection prompt:
'For JOOLA specifically, classify detection source:
  - paddle: JOOLA logo on paddle face = confirmed product use
  - net: JOOLA logo on net = venue sponsorship, NOT product use
  - venue: JOOLA on walls/banners = facility sponsorship
Only count paddle detections as brand_category: paddle'

all_findings.title: Viral arc: 'top_play' is 130.0x more likely in top clips (13.0% vs 0.0%)
all_findings.evidence: Story arc 'top_play' appears in 13.0% of top-viral clips but only 0.0% of bottom-viral clips.
all_findings.action: Prioritize 'top_play' clips in automated highlight reels

all_findings.title: Longitudinal tracking possible: 54 players with 5+ clips
all_findings.evidence: 54 players have enough data for improvement tracking. Top: PickleBill(411), Chintan(262), Daniella(110), Coach_Block(106), Ironvarr(88)
all_findings.action: Build player improvement trajectory product — track skill changes over time

all_findings.title: Signature moves = viral gold: 1558 clips with signatures avg 3.1 viral vs 3.9
all_findings.evidence: 1558 clips have identified signature moves, averaging 3.1 viral vs 3.9 for clips without.
all_findings.action: Auto-tag signature move clips as premium content

all_findings.title: Brand-skill split: goomba users excel at kitchen (7.7), R&M_foz_wifi_goomba users at power (5.1)
all_findings.evidence: Among brands with 10+ clips: goomba has highest avg kitchen mastery (7.7), while R&M_foz_wifi_goomba has highest power rating (5.1).
all_findings.action: Build brand-skill correlation report for brand partnerships

all_findings.title: Top content creators: PickleBill leads with 411 clips
all_findings.evidence:
PickleBill: 411 clips, viral=3.1, All-Rounder
Chintan: 262 clips, viral=3.2, Dink Specialist
Daniella: 110 clips, viral=3.6, Kitchen Master
Coach_Block: 106 clips, viral=2.9, Kitchen Master
Ironvarr: 88 clips, viral=3.4, Kitchen Master
all_findings.action: Create player spotlight feature for top contributors

all_findings.title: Skill standout: Ajr averages 8.5 in consistency across 6 clips
all_findings.evidence: Ajr skill profile: consistency=8.5, kitchen=8.2, touch=8.0, composure=7.5, court_iq=7.0, court_coverage=6.2, athleticism=5.2, power=3.0, creativity=3.0
all_findings.action: Feature Ajr in coaching product showcase

all_findings.title: Coach Certification Marketplace
all_findings.evidence: Use Idea Lab to automatically certify coaches: flag which coaches' pupils improve fastest, which techniques generate the best outcomes, which feedback gets adopted. Sell 'Certified by Courtana' badge. Coaches list themselves on our marketplace. We take 15% commission on coaching bookings + training sales. Creates economic incentive for coaches to use our system, which feeds better data, which certifies better coaches.
all_findings.action: Evaluate: monetization_novel play

all_findings.title: Viral rally length: top clips avg 17 shots vs bottom 13
all_findings.evidence: Top-viral clips average 17.1 shots per rally, bottom-viral average 13.1.
all_findings.action: Investigate optimal rally length range for highlight reels

all_findings.title: Social caption generation at 99.9% — ready to productize
all_findings.evidence: Social media captions are generated for 1586/1588 clips. This is already a viable content-as-a-service product.
all_findings.action: Create API endpoint for social caption generation — charge per clip

all_findings.title: Best predicted badges: victory spin (F1=1.0), wall of hands (F1=1.0), dink master (F1=1.0)
all_findings.evidence: Top performing badges by F1 score: victory spin (F1=1.0), wall of hands (F1=1.0), dink master (F1=1.0)
all_findings.action: Use these badges as confidence anchors for the prediction system

all_findings.title: Sport-Agnostic AI as a Service (Pickleball → Tennis → Badminton → Pickleball Data Licensing)
all_findings.evidence: Our shot analysis and skill rating engine is sport-agnostic. License it to tennis facilities, badminton clubs, etc. Collect massive cross-sport movement + equipment data. Then sell BACK to pickleball: 'Here's how tennis forehands compare to pickleball drives.' Create a unified athlete data standard. Pickleball becomes the hub of a multi-sport analytics ecosystem. Brands see cross-sport equipment trends first.
all_findings.action: Evaluate: network_effect play

product_concept.name: BadgeBoost: Equipment Sponsorship Gap Filler
product_concept.first_screen: Dashboard showing 5 unbranded equipment categories (shoes, paddles, balls, nets, bags) with side-by-side comparison: current AI detection accuracy (showing missed badges) vs. improved model with positive examples. Red/green indicators for each category.
product_concept.why_powerful: Turns detection gaps into immediate sponsorship opportunities by showing brands exactly which equipment categories their competitors aren't capturing in sports footage.

product_concept.name: CourtShare: True Sponsorship Value Calculator
product_concept.first_screen: Input screen showing venue/event name, followed by real-time breakdown: raw brand mention counts vs. adjusted 'court-presence score' that filters out net/venue inflation, displaying 3 major brands' true market share with percentage differences highlighted.
product_concept.why_powerful: Eliminates sponsor overpayment by revealing which brands are inflated by venue branding rather than actual equipment presence, letting sponsors negotiate based on true exposure.

product_concept.name: JOOLAfix: Intelligent Brand Disambiguation Engine
product_concept.first_screen: Clip review interface showing JOOLA detection confidence score (215/287 verified clips displayed as success rate), with toggle to view disambiguation rules (paddle logo vs. ball branding vs. apparel logos) and ability to flag false positives from the unverified 72 clips.
product_concept.why_powerful: Surfaces the most critical brand in the whitespace categories while solving its specific detection problem, making JOOLA and competitors' true court presence measurable and monetizable.
